title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Haemul Pajeon Recipe: Authentic Korean Seafood Pancake
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Haemul Pajeon, the crispy Korean seafood pancake, at home with our easy step-by-step recipe. Perfect for a delicious meal!
seo.h1: Authentic Haemul Pajeon: Korean Seafood Pancake Recipe
h2Headings: Ingredients You Need for Haemul Pajeon
Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Haemul Pajeon
Pro Tips for the Best Crispy Haemul Pajeon
seo.imageAlt: Crispy Haemul Pajeon, a traditional Korean seafood pancake served with a savory dipping sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a taste of Korea with this delicious homemade Haemul Pajeon, packed with fresh seafood and green onions.

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved Korean savory pancake, celebrated for its crispy edges and tender, flavorful interior packed with fresh seafood and aromatic green onions. This dish offers a delightful textural contrast, making it a perfect appetizer or light meal. Enjoy the authentic taste of Korea with this easy-to-follow recipe, bringing the vibrant flavors of the sea to your table.

ingredients.notes: cleaned and cut into bite-sized pieces

instructions.title: Prepare the Batter
instructions.text: In a large bowl, whisk together all-purpose flour, rice flour, salt, egg, and cold water until a smooth batter forms. Ensure there are no lumps. Set aside to rest for 10 minutes while you prepare other ingredients. This resting period helps develop gluten for a better texture.

instructions.title: Prepare Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Clean and pat dry the mixed seafood, cutting any larger pieces into bite-sized portions. Slice the green onions into 3-4 inch lengths and thinly slice the onion. If using, prepare the red chili slices for garnish. Arrange all prepared ingredients within easy reach.

instructions.title: Make the Dipping Sauce
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, sugar, toasted sesame seeds, and minced garlic. Stir well until the sugar dissolves. Taste and adjust seasonings if necessary. This sauce can be prepared in advance.

instructions.title: Heat the Pan and Add Batter
instructions.text: Heat a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add 2-3 tablespoons of vegetable oil, swirling to coat the bottom. Pour about 1/2 cup of batter into the hot pan, spreading it thinly to form a round pancake. You should hear a sizzling sound.

instructions.title: Layer Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Quickly arrange a generous amount of green onions, sliced onion, and mixed seafood evenly over the wet batter in the pan. Gently press them into the batter. Drizzle a little more batter over the toppings to help bind them. Cook for 4-5 minutes until the bottom is golden brown and crispy.

instructions.title: Flip and Finish Cooking
instructions.text: Drizzle another tablespoon of oil around the edges of the pancake. Carefully flip the pancake using a large spatula. Cook for another 3-5 minutes on the second side until golden brown and the seafood is cooked through. The pancake should be crispy on both sides.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board. Cut into wedges or squares. Repeat the process with the remaining batter and fillings, adding more oil as needed for each pancake. Serve hot with the prepared dipping sauce.

tips: For extra crispiness, use a combination of all-purpose flour and rice flour, and ensure your pan is very hot before adding the batter. A small amount of ice water in the batter can also contribute to a crispier texture.
Do not overcrowd the pan with too many toppings; this can make the pancake soggy. Arrange the green onions and seafood evenly but ensure they have contact with the batter.
Serve Haemul Pajeon immediately after cooking to enjoy its optimal crispy texture. It pairs wonderfully with makgeolli (Korean rice wine) or a cold beer.
article:
Korean cuisine is a vibrant tapestry of flavors, textures, and aromas, and among its most cherished dishes is the savory pancake. While many might be familiar with the simple jeon (pancake), one particular variant stands out for its incredible depth of flavor and satisfying chewiness: Haemul Pajeon. This iconic Korean seafood pancake is a culinary delight, boasting crispy edges, a tender interior, and a generous medley of fresh seafood and aromatic scallions. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a curious home cook, mastering Haemul Pajeon is a rewarding journey that brings a piece of Korea's dynamic food culture right into your kitchen. Get ready to impress your taste buds and your guests with this comprehensive guide to crafting the perfect Haemul Pajeon.

## What is Haemul Pajeon?

Haemul Pajeon literally translates to "seafood scallion pancake," with "haemul" meaning seafood and "pajeon" referring to a scallion pancake. It’s far more than just a simple pancake; it's a symphony of textures and tastes. Imagine a crispy, golden-brown exterior giving way to a soft, chewy interior studded with succulent shrimp, tender squid, and plenty of vibrant green scallions. The batter itself is subtly seasoned, allowing the natural sweetness of the seafood and the fresh bite of the scallions to shine through. This beloved dish is a staple on Korean restaurant menus, a popular street food snack, and a comforting meal often enjoyed with makgeolli (Korean rice wine), especially on a rainy day. Its comforting warmth and satisfying flavors make it a perfect appetizer, side dish, or even a light main course. The sheer popularity of Haemul Pajeon is a testament to its irresistible charm and deliciousness.

## Ingredients You Need for Haemul Pajeon

Creating an authentic Haemul Pajeon requires a thoughtful selection of fresh ingredients. Here’s what you’ll need:

For the Batter:
*   1 cup all-purpose flour (or Korean pancake mix for convenience)
*   2 tablespoons cornstarch (for extra crispiness)
*   1 large egg
*   1 ¼ cups ice-cold water or anchovy broth (for deeper umami)
*   ½ teaspoon salt
*   1 tablespoon soy sauce

For the Seafood:
*   ½ pound mixed seafood, prepared (e.g., shrimp, squid, mussels). For shrimp, peel, devein, and chop into bite-sized pieces. For squid, clean, score lightly, and cut into rings or strips.
*   Optional: a handful of small oysters or chopped clams for extra flavor.

For the Vegetables:
*   1 bunch green onions (about 8-10 stalks), cut into 2-3 inch pieces. Separate some for mixing into the batter and some for arranging on top.
*   ½ small onion, thinly sliced
*   ½ small carrot, julienned
*   1-2 green chilies (like serrano or jalapeño), thinly sliced (optional, for a kick)
*   Optional: ½ red bell pepper, thinly sliced for color

For the Dipping Sauce:
*   3 tablespoons soy sauce
*   1 tablespoon rice vinegar
*   1 teaspoon sesame oil
*   ½ teaspoon gochugaru (Korean chili flakes)
*   1 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds
*   1 clove garlic, minced (optional)

## Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Haemul Pajeon

Follow these steps to craft your own delicious Korean seafood pancake:

1.  **Prepare the Dipping Sauce:** In a small bowl, combine all dipping sauce ingredients. Stir well and set aside to allow the flavors to meld.
2.  **Prepare Seafood and Vegetables:** Thoroughly clean and prepare your chosen seafood as described above. Slice all vegetables. Keep the green onion pieces separated into two portions: one for mixing into the batter and one for arranging on top of the pancake.
3.  **Make the Batter:** In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, cornstarch, and salt. In a separate bowl, lightly beat the egg with the ice-cold water (or broth) and soy sauce. Gradually pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients, whisking until just combined. A few lumps are okay; do not overmix, as this can lead to a tough pancake. The batter should be thin, like a crepe batter.
4.  **Combine Ingredients:** Gently fold the prepared seafood, sliced onion, julienned carrot, and the first portion of green onions into the batter.
5.  **Cook the Pancake:** Heat a large (10-12 inch) non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add 2-3 tablespoons of cooking oil (vegetable or canola) and swirl to coat the pan generously. Once the oil is hot, pour about half of the batter mixture into the pan, spreading it evenly to form a thin, round pancake. Immediately arrange the remaining green onion pieces and any additional seafood (like whole shrimp or squid rings) artfully on top of the batter.
6.  **Achieve Crispiness:** Cook for 4-6 minutes, or until the bottom is golden brown and crispy, and the edges begin to curl. Drizzle a little more oil around the edges of the pancake to ensure even crisping.
7.  **Flip and Finish:** Carefully flip the pancake. You can use two spatulas or slide it onto a large plate and then invert it back into the pan. Cook for another 3-5 minutes on the second side until golden brown and the seafood is cooked through.
8.  **Serve:** Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board. Cut it into wedges or squares. Repeat with the remaining batter. Serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce.

## Pro Tips for the Best Crispy Haemul Pajeon

Achieving that perfect, restaurant-quality crispy Haemul Pajeon requires a few tricks. These pro tips will elevate your pancake from good to unforgettable:

*   **Ice-Cold Batter is Key:** Always use ice-cold water (or broth) for your batter. This inhibits gluten development, resulting in a lighter, crispier pancake. Some even add an ice cube to the batter while it rests.
*   **Don't Overmix:** Overmixing develops gluten, which makes the pancake tough and chewy instead of crispy. A few lumps in the batter are perfectly fine.
*   **Generous Oil:** Don't be shy with the oil! A good amount of oil in the pan is crucial for achieving that desirable crispy crust. You can even drizzle a little extra around the edges while it cooks.
*   **Medium-High Heat:** Maintain a consistent medium-high heat. Too low, and the pancake will become soggy; too high, and it will burn before cooking through.
*   **Thin is In:** Spread the batter relatively thin in the pan. Thicker pancakes tend to be softer and less crispy.
*   **Even Distribution:** Ensure the seafood and scallions are evenly distributed throughout the batter and on top for a balanced bite in every piece.
*   **Flip Once:** Resist the urge to constantly flip the pancake. Let it cook undisturbed on one side until it's deeply golden and crispy before flipping it just once.

## Serving Suggestions & Dipping Sauce

Haemul Pajeon is best enjoyed hot, straight from the pan. Cut it into bite-sized wedges or squares immediately after cooking. Garnish with a sprinkle of extra sesame seeds or thinly sliced green onions for a pop of freshness.

The traditional and most beloved pairing for Haemul Pajeon is makgeolli, a lightly sparkling Korean rice wine. Its slightly sweet and tangy profile beautifully complements the savory richness of the pancake. Cold beer or soju are also excellent choices. Beyond beverages, Haemul Pajeon makes a fantastic appetizer for a larger Korean meal, a satisfying snack, or a light lunch. The accompanying dipping sauce is non-negotiable; its tangy, savory, and slightly spicy notes cut through the richness of the pancake, enhancing every bite.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Haemul Pajeon is a masterpiece, feel free to experiment with variations to suit your taste or what you have on hand:

*   **Vegetarian Pajeon:** Omit the seafood entirely and bulk up on vegetables. Mushrooms (oyster, shiitake), zucchini, bell peppers, or even potato strips make excellent additions.
*   **Kimchi Pajeon:** For a spicy and tangy twist, add about ½ cup of chopped, well-squeezed kimchi and a tablespoon or two of its juice to the batter. This creates a vibrant and flavorful Kimchi Jeon.
*   **Meat Pajeon:** Incorporate thinly sliced, marinated beef (bulgogi style) or pork for a heartier pancake.
*   **Different Seafood Combinations:** Don't limit yourself to shrimp and squid. Crab meat, small scallops, or even a mix of various fish flakes can be used.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of gochugaru in the dipping sauce, or add a pinch to the batter itself for more heat.
*   **Gluten-Free Option:** Use a gluten-free all-purpose flour blend in place of regular flour for a gluten-free version.

Mastering Haemul Pajeon at home is incredibly rewarding. This delightful Korean seafood pancake offers a perfect balance of savory flavors, satisfying textures, and fresh ingredients. Whether you're making it for a special occasion or simply craving a taste of Korea, following these steps and tips will help you create a truly authentic and delicious Haemul Pajeon that will transport your taste buds straight to the bustling streets of Seoul. Enjoy your culinary adventure!
